hook.text: Agile replaced 200-page requirements docs with simple index cards that start with 'As a user, I want.'

buildup.text: User stories describe what someone needs, not how to build it. Intent over specification.

discovery.text: Stories are invitations for conversation — the real requirements emerge through dialogue.

twist.text: Many teams treat user stories as mini-specs, losing the conversation. The format isn't the point.

climax.text: User stories shifted focus from 'what the system does' to 'what the user needs.'

punchline.text: The best requirements fit on an index card.

quiz.question: What is the purpose of a user story in Agile?
options: To start a conversation about what the user needs
